summary: This checklist addresses missing provider support (ZAI, Kimi, Hive LLM), incorrect authentication command arguments for Antigravity, and missing model verification logic for OpenRouter within the setup script.

items.verification_point: Check for detection of Claude, Codex, ZAI, Kimi, and Hive credentials.
items.expected_outcome: The script detects ~/.claude/.credentials.json, ~/.codex/auth.json, ZAI_API_KEY, ~/.kimi/config.toml, and HIVE_API_KEY.

items.verification_point: Verify support for zai_code, kimi_code, and hive_llm sub-modes in previous settings.
items.expected_outcome: The logic recognizes use_kimi_code_subscription, use_zai_code_subscription, and hive_llm provider types.

items.verification_point: Check the arguments passed to the antigravity_auth.py script.
items.expected_outcome: The command includes 'auth account add' to trigger the OAuth flow (e.g., & $UvCmd run python ... auth account add).

items.verification_point: Check for specific api_base and api_key_env_var mappings for ZAI, Kimi, and Hive LLM.
items.expected_outcome: Switch cases set api.z.ai for ZAI, api.kimi.com/coding for Kimi, and appropriate environment variable keys for all three.

items.verification_point: Verify the presence of OpenRouter model ID normalization and verification logic.
items.expected_outcome: The script implements Normalize-OpenRouterModelId and uses scripts/check_llm_key.py to validate OpenRouter IDs.

items.verification_point: Check for interactive validation and health checks for ZAI, Kimi, and Hive keys.
items.expected_outcome: Interactive loops use check_llm_key.py with retry logic for these subscription-based providers.
